model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Tennessee holds a significant analytical advantage with superior KenPom (15 vs 93) and Torvik (15 vs 87) rankings, featuring elite defensive efficiency (rank 13) that should contain Miami (OH)'s offense. While the RedHawks have an impressive 31-1 record and momentum from their First Four win over SMU, their MAC schedule lacks the quality of Tennessee's SEC battles, and the Volunteers' tournament experience under Rick Barnes provides a crucial edge. The expected return of star freshman Nate Ament from his ankle injury further strengthens Tennessee's position in this classic 6/11 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Tennessee vs Miami (OH) Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Tennessee is the clear favorite based on analytical metrics and tournament experience, though Miami (OH) presents upset potential with their exceptional record and recent momentum.
evidence: Tennessee ranks 15th in both KenPom and Torvik with a 22-11 record, while Miami (OH) ranks 93rd/87th with a 31-1 record
Tennessee's defensive efficiency (rank 13) is significantly better than Miami's (rank 149)
Miami (OH) just defeated SMU 89-79 in the First Four for their first NCAA tournament win since 1999
Tennessee's Nate Ament is expected to play after practicing with his sprained ankle, though he's still receiving treatment
Miami (OH) has played a weak MAC schedule (strength of schedule significantly lower than Tennessee's SEC schedule)
Tennessee has tournament experience under Rick Barnes, while Miami (OH) is making their first tournament appearance since 1999
